title: adinandra dubia
intro: Adinandra dubia, also known as the Wild Olive or Black Olive, is a species of flowering plant in the family Actinidiaceae. The plant is native to Southeast Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. It is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving cough and sore throat, and treating digestive issues. Adinandra dubia can be prepared as a herbal tea, decoction, or infusion, and its leaves and fruits are also used in folk medicine to treat various ailments.
